research_findings: The next-stage lightweight committee flow is mostly implemented now, but the final baseline still needs one explicit closeout step: prove the advisory-mode safety boundary in validation and refresh the baseline docs to match the shipped system.
The highest-value final improvement is to test that advisory implementation mode still cannot report without evaluator pass, then update the baseline summaries.
This version should avoid new features and end the session with a clean explainable baseline.
committee_feedback: Product Council: the session should end by proving the new flow rather than adding one more mechanism.
Architecture Council: advisory implementation mode must be explicitly tested as non-authoritative for report safety.
Operator Council: the baseline docs should describe the upgraded lightweight committee flow as it exists now, not as a roadmap fragment.

research_gate.summary: Close the session by validating the advisory-mode safety boundary and refreshing baseline docs for the upgraded lightweight committee flow.

product_council.summary: Finish the session by proving the strongest new control boundary and documenting the actual baseline clearly.
product_council.decision: Approve a closeout version focused on validation and baseline docs only.

architecture_council.summary: Add one more validation path for advisory-mode safety, then refresh the baseline summaries to match the shipped system.
architecture_council.decision: Do not introduce new protocol mechanics in the closeout version.

operator_council.summary: The next session should inherit a repo that already explains what is true, what is strict, and what remains open.
operator_council.decision: Approve if the closeout remains factual and concise.

delivery_secretary.summary: The repo now needs proof and explanation more than more mechanics.
delivery_secretary.next_action: Validate advisory-mode report blocking and refresh the baseline docs.

audit_secretary.summary: The closeout should verify that implementation advisory mode does not weaken report-time evaluator-pass enforcement.
audit_secretary.decision_record: The session ends cleanly once the control boundary is tested and the baseline documents describe the upgraded lightweight flow accurately.

scope_decision.selected_goal: Extend validation and baseline docs for the next-stage lightweight committee enhancements
scope_decision.why_selected: The upgraded lightweight committee flow is in place; the remaining high-value work is to prove the final safety boundary and document the actual baseline cleanly.
scope_in: Extend validate-kit to prove advisory implementation mode still blocks report without pass.
Refresh README and PLANS baseline summaries.
Close the session with a clean explainable baseline.
scope_out: Do not add new mechanics or gates.
Do not expand the protocol beyond current shipped behavior.
stop_conditions: Stop if the closeout starts introducing new mechanics instead of documenting and validating the current baseline.
dissent: Keep the baseline refresh concise and factual.
scope_decision.next_action: End the session and start a new backlog only when new committee capabilities are worth pursuing.

critique: This is the right end-of-session version: prove the boundary and leave the repo easy to resume.
minimum_fixes_required: Keep the closeout focused on validation and baseline accuracy.

review_state.goal_title: Extend validation and baseline docs for the next-stage lightweight committee enhancements
